summary: The main claim is: Specifically because I think that sandboxes like an improved WASM could make it such that conditioned on careful data curation, that side channels have 0 probability to give the AI any information outside of what we give it.
I also believe that this can be done with no performance penalty (unlike a lot of security measures.) My argument follows in 2 steps: 1.
The main claim is: My impression had been that the generalization capacity of neural networks would be more sensitive to their inductive biases than that.
My opinion: (see below) What can linearized neural networks actually say about generalization?
The main claim is: Q: I can't believe you can have just a single value.
Like money: it has some personal value for you, but it also has some value outside of your head.
The main claim is: This means that we should expect shit to not work, unless something forces it into the narrow set of states that actually work and do something.
